title: Model-Based Reinforcement Learning
summary: Model-Based Reinforcement Learning belongs to the Reinforcement Learning area of AI/ML. It sits inside Reinforcement Learning. Start after Reinforcement Learning Algorithms and Q-Learning. It opens into Rule-Based Reinforcement Learning and Value-Based Reinforcement Learning.

blocks.title: Compare and Contrast

panels.label: What it is
panels.body: Model-Based Reinforcement Learning belongs to the Reinforcement Learning area of AI/ML.

panels.label: What it is not
panels.body: It is not just Value-Based Reinforcement Learning and Rule-Based Reinforcement Learning; the graph shows how it sits inside Reinforcement Learning.

panels.label: Common confusion
panels.body: Ask whether the term changes policy, reward, exploration, or control dynamics. Compare it against Value-Based Reinforcement Learning and Rule-Based Reinforcement Learning so the boundary stays visible instead of collapsing into a synonym pile.

panels.label: Best next step
panels.body: Review Reinforcement Learning Algorithms and Q-Learning first, then move toward Rule-Based Reinforcement Learning and Value-Based Reinforcement Learning.
